questions.text: Which of the following has lower vapor pressure?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Glycerol has lowest vapour pressure.
Isopentane has highest vapour pressure.
Vapour pressure of ethanol is gretaer than glycerol.
Glycerol has lowest vapour pressure. Ethanol has greater vapour pressure than glycerol.

questions.explanation: Correct option is A. Vapor pressure depends upon the molecules that bond well and the forces of attraction of mercury metal atoms are quite strong. So, with the help of this relation we are able to find the lowest vapor pressure. Vapor pressure can be explained as: the pressure exerted by the gaseous form of liquid on the liquid itself when both are in equilibrium or in other words we can say that it is the ability of a compound to bond with itself. Compound molecules that bond well with each other will have low vapor pressure whereas poorly bonded compounds will have high vapor pressure. The temperature at which the vapor pressure at the surface of a liquid becomes equal to the pressure exerted by the surroundings is known as the boiling point of the liquid. There are three common factors which influence the vapor pressure are surface area, intermolecular forces and temperature. At different temperatures different molecules have different vapor pressure. Vapor pressure increases with increase in temperature The forces between the individual metal atoms of mercury are quite strong as compared to the cohesive molecular forces such as hydrogen bonding which is present in the case of alcohols, ethers and water. Other options B C and D are wrong as values of vapor pressure of different substances are given below :

questions.text: Greater the number of moles, _ will be the number of molecules.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: Lesser. The number of molecules will not be lesser if the number of moles is greater. In fact, the number of molecules will be greater if the number of moles is greater.
Moderate. The number of molecules will not be moderate if the number of moles is greater. The number of molecules will either be greater or equal to the number of moles.

The greater the number of moles, the greater will be the number of molecules. This is because a mole is a unit of measurement that is equal to the number of atoms or molecules in a sample of a substance. The number of atoms or molecules in a mole is a constant, so the greater the number of moles, the greater the number of atoms or molecules.For example, if you have 1 mole of water, you will have 6.022 x 1023 water molecules. If you have 2 moles of water, you will have 2 x 6.022 x 1023 = 1.2044 x 1024 water molecules.
Equal. The number of molecules will not be equal to the number of moles if the number of moles is greater. The number of molecules will always be greater than or equal to the number of moles.

questions.explanation: The greater the number of moles, the greater will be the number of molecules. This is because a mole is a unit of measurement that is equal to the number of atoms or molecules in a sample of a substance. The number of atoms or molecules in a mole is a constant, so the greater the number of moles, the greater the number of atoms or molecules.For example, if you have 1 mole of water, you will have 6.022 x 1023 water molecules. If you have 2 moles of water, you will have 2 x 6.022 x 1023 = 1.2044 x 1024 water molecules.

questions.text: Which of the following isotopes of carbon is/are used in carbon dating?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: False.
False.

Carbon-14 is a radioactive isotope of carbon that is used in carbon dating, also known as radiocarbon dating. The method works by measuring the amount of carbon-14 in a sample of organic material, such as wood, bone, or cloth, and comparing it to the initial amount of carbon-14 in the atmosphere.
False.

questions.explanation: Carbon-14 is a radioactive isotope of carbon that is used in carbon dating, also known as radiocarbon dating. The method works by measuring the amount of carbon-14 in a sample of organic material, such as wood, bone, or cloth, and comparing it to the initial amount of carbon-14 in the atmosphere.

questions.text: The number of charges present on a cation depends on the number of electrons _ by the atom.

If electrons are lost, a cation is formed. Hence, this option is the correct one.

questions.text: The reactant which is consumed completely in a reaction is known as a _ reactant.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: "Fractional" does not describe a type of reactant in a chemical reaction.
"Initial" refers to the starting amount or concentration of a reactant in a reaction, but it does not describe the reactant that is consumed completely.

A limiting reactant is a reactant that is present in the smallest amount in a reaction and therefore limits the amount of product that can be formed. It is called the "limiting" reactant because it determines the extent of the reaction.
"Minor" does not describe the reactant that is consumed completely in a reaction. The minor reactant may not be completely consumed and may be present in the final product as an impurity.

questions.explanation: A limiting reactant is a reactant that is present in the smallest amount in a reaction and therefore limits the amount of product that can be formed. It is called the "limiting" reactant because it determines the extent of the reaction.

questions.text: Which of the following process is used to improve the quality of gasoline?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Reforming is used to improve the quality of gasoline because it converts low-octane naphtha into high-octane reformates. Octane rating is a measure of a fuel's ability to resist knocking, which is an uncontrolled combustion that can damage an engine. High-octane gasoline is less likely to knock than low-octane gasoline, so it is better for performance and fuel economy.

questions.text: The geometrical isomers in which similar groups on double bond carbon atoms are present on opposite sides are called as _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Only in cis-trans isomers, there is a double bondbetween the carbon atoms having bulky groups on either side. When the two similar groups are opposite to each other, this forms a trans-isomer hence Option A is the correct answer.

In organic chemistry, an alkane, or paraffin, is an acyclic saturated hydrocarbon. In other words, an alkane consists of hydrogen and carbon atoms arranged in a tree structure in which all the carbon–carbon bonds are single. Alkanes have the general chemical formula CₙH₂ₙ₊₂.

Cis isomers are molecules with the same connectivity of atoms. They feature same side groups placed on the same side of a double bond. Trans isomers feature molecules with same side groups placed on opposite sides of a double bond.

Positional isomers are constitutional isomers with the same carbon skeleton and will have the same functional group. They differ from each other in the position of the functional groups on the carbon chain and the phenomenon is known as positional isomerism.

questions.explanation: Only in cis-trans isomers, there is a double bond between the carbon atoms having bulky groups on either side. When the two similar groups are opposite to each other, this forms a trans-isomer hence Option A is the correct answer.

questions.text: Tautomerism involves the transfer of _ ?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is wrong because tautomerism doesn't involve the transfer of electrons. It involves the transfer of hydrogen atoms.
This option is also wrong because tautomerism doesn't involve any transfer of any carbon atom.
This option is also wrong because there is no shifting of any functional group in tautomerism. In tautomerism, there is a shifting of hydrogen atoms.

Tautomerism, is the existence of two or more chemical compounds that are capable of facile interconversion, in many cases merely exchanging a hydrogen atom between two other atoms, to either of which it forms a covalent bond(fact).

questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because tautomerism doesn't involve the transfer of electrons. It involves the transfer of hydrogen atoms. Option B is also wrong because tautomerism doesn't involve any transfer of any carbon atom. Option C is also wrong because there is no shifting of any functional group in tautomerism. In tautomerism, there is a shifting of hydrogen atoms. Option : D Tautomerism, is the existence of two or more chemical compounds that are capable of facile interconversion, in many cases merely exchanging a hydrogen atom between two other atoms, to either of which it forms a covalent bond(fact).

questions.text: If a double bond is present between two carbons then this class of compounds in called as _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkanes are saturated compounds and have single bonds between carbons.Example: methane
Alkynes are a homologous series of hydrocarbons containing triple bonds between carbon atoms.Example: ethyne
In a carbonyl group, a double bond is present between carbon and oxygen.
Alkenes are a homologous series of hydrocarbons containing one or more double bonds between carbon atoms.

questions.explanation: Alkenes are the correct answer because they specifically contain carbon-carbon double bonds, distinguishing them from alkanes, alkynes, and carbonyl compounds. Alkanes have only single bonds, alkynes have triple bonds, and carbonyl compounds have carbon-oxygen double bonds.

questions.text: Which of the following isomerism is shown by alkynes?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkynes can exhibit positional isomerism where the position of the triple bond changes within the carbon chain. For example, in the case of C4H6, you can have 1-butyne and 2-butyne, which are positional isomers.

This type of isomerism occurs in compounds with restricted rotation around a double bond. Alkynes do not typically exhibit this type of isomerism because they have a linear structure with a triple bond, which doesn't allow for the same kind of rotation as a double bond.
This type of isomerism occurs in compounds with different spatial arrangements of substituents around a double bond. Alkynes, due to their linear structure, do not usually show cis-trans isomerism.

This type of isomerism involves compounds with the same molecular formula but different functional groups. Alkynes do not exhibit this type of isomerism because they have a specific functional group, the triple bond.

questions.explanation: Alkynes can exhibit positional isomerism where the position of the triple bond changes within the carbon chain. For example, in the case of C4H6, you can have 1-butyne and 2-butyne, which are positional isomers.

questions.text: Which of the following does not have sp2 hybridized orbital?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Acetone has a carbonyl group (C=O) and a methyl group (-CH3). The carbon in the carbonyl group is sp2 hybridized, while the carbon in the methyl group is sp3 hybridized.

Acetonitrile has a nitrile group (-CN), where the carbon is sp hybridized. The nitrogen is also sp hybridized. Since the carbon in the nitrile group is sp hybridized, it does not have sp2 hybridized orbitals.
Acetic acid has a carbonyl group (C=O) and a hydroxyl group (-OH). The carbon in the carbonyl group is sp2 hybridized, while the carbon in the hydroxyl group is sp3 hybridized.
Acetamide has an amide group (-CONH2). The carbon in the carbonyl group is sp2 hybridized, while the carbon in the amide group is sp3 hybridized.

questions.explanation: Acetonitrile has a nitrile group (-CN), where the carbon is sp hybridized. The nitrogen is also sp hybridized. Since the carbon in the nitrile group is sp hybridized, it does not have sp2 hybridized orbitals.

questions.text: Which of the following is correct about the "octane number"?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Higher the octane number, higher will be the knocking.
options.explanation: Higher octane numbers in gasoline indicate that the fuel is more resistant to knocking, which is a type of uncontrolled combustion that can occur in spark-ignition engines. Therefore, higher octane numbers actually mean lower knocking.

options.text: Higher the octane number, the efficiency of fuel will be lower.
options.explanation: Higher octane numbers in gasoline indicate that the fuel is more resistant to knocking, which is a type of uncontrolled combustion that can occur in spark-ignition engines. Using a fuel with an appropriate octane rating for your engine can improve fuel efficiency and performance.

options.text: Higher the octane number, lower the knocking.
options.explanation: Higher octane numbers in gasoline indicate that the fuel is more resistant to knocking, which is a type of uncontrolled combustion that can occur in spark-ignition engines. Knocking can cause engine damage and reduce performance, so it's important to use fuels with the appropriate octane rating for your engine.

options.text: Lower the octane number, lower the knocking.
options.explanation: Lower octane numbers in gasoline indicate that the fuel is more prone to knocking, which is a type of uncontrolled combustion that can occur in spark-ignition engines. Therefore, lower octane numbers actually mean higher knocking.

questions.explanation: Higher octane numbers in gasoline indicate that the fuel is more resistant to knocking, which is a type of uncontrolled combustion that can occur in spark-ignition engines. Knocking can cause engine damage and reduce performance, so it's important to use fuels with the appropriate octane rating for your engine.

questions.text: The organic compounds having all C-C single bonds are called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Organic compounds with all carbon-carbon single bonds are called alkanes. They have the general formula CnH2n+2, where n is the number of carbon atoms in the molecule. Alkanes are saturated hydrocarbons, meaning they contain the maximum number of hydrogen atoms possible for their carbon skeleton.
Alkenes are unsaturated hydrocarbons containing at least one carbon-carbon double bond. Unlike alkanes, they are not saturated with hydrogen atoms because of the presence of the double bond.

Alkynes are unsaturated hydrocarbons containing at least one carbon-carbon triple bond. Like alkenes, they are also unsaturated and contain fewer hydrogen atoms compared to alkanes due to the presence of multiple bonds.
Option a is correct so all other options cannot be correct.

questions.explanation: Organic compounds with all carbon-carbon single bonds are called alkanes. They have the general formula CnH2n+2, where n is the number of carbon atoms in the molecule. Alkanes are saturated hydrocarbons, meaning they contain the maximum number of hydrogen atoms possible for their carbon skeleton.

questions.text: Which of the following isomerism is shown by alkynes?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkynes can exhibit positional isomerism where the position of the triple bond changes within the carbon chain. For example, in the case of C4H6, you can have 1-butyne and 2-butyne, which are positional isomers.

This type of isomerism occurs in compounds with restricted rotation around a double bond. Alkynes do not typically exhibit this type of isomerism because they have a linear structure with a triple bond, which doesn't allow for the same kind of rotation as a double bond.
This type of isomerism occurs in compounds with different spatial arrangements of substituents around a double bond. Alkynes, due to their linear structure, do not usually show cis-trans isomerism.

This type of isomerism involves compounds with the same molecular formula but different functional groups. Alkynes do not exhibit this type of isomerism because they have a specific functional group, the triple bond.

questions.explanation: Alkynes can exhibit positional isomerism where the position of the triple bond changes within the carbon chain. For example, in the case of C4H6, you can have 1-butyne and 2-butyne, which are positional isomers.

questions.text: The isomerism in which compounds have different number of carbon atoms on both sides of the functional group is called as?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Tautomerism is a phenomenon where a single chemical compound tends to exist in two or more interconvertible structures that are different in terms of the relative position of one atomic nucleus, which is generally the hydrogen. Hence, it is an incorrect option.

Metamerism is a type of isomerism that arises due to the unequal distribution of carbon atoms on either side of the functional group. Such compounds belong to the same homologous series. For example, diethyl ether and methyl- propyl ether are metamers.

Geometric isomers are two or more coordination compounds which contain the same number and types of atoms, and bonds (i.e., the connectivity between atoms is the same), but which have different spatial arrangements of the atoms. Hence, it is an incorrect option.

Positional isomers are constitutional isomers with the same carbon skeleton and will have the same functional group. They differ from each other in the position of the functional groups on the carbon chain and the phenomenon is known as positional isomerism. Hence, it is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: Metamerism is a type of isomerism that arises due to the unequal distribution of carbon atoms on either side of the functional group. Such compounds belong to the same homologous series. For example, diethyl ether and methyl ,-propyl ether are metamers.

questions.text: The crude petroleum is separated in fraction by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Filtration is the process by which solid particles are separated from a liquid or gas. However, crude petroleum is all liquid. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Fractional distillation is the process by which a mixture of liquids is separated from each other on the basis of their boiling points. It is the most suitable method of separating the fractions of crude oil. Hence, this option is the correct option.

Steam distillation is the process by which volatile compounds are extracted from heat-sensitive materials. It is used for extracting essential oils. However, it does not work for crude oil. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Fractional sublimation is the process by which a solid substance is converted to vapors, without passing through a liquid state. It is used to separate mixtures of solids, however, crude petroleum is a mixture of liquids. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Crude petroleum contains many components with different boiling points. Fractional distillation separates them by heating and condensing at specific temperature ranges. This method efficiently isolates fuels like petrol, kerosene, and diesel.

questions.text: Ethers show the phenomenon of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Compounds that can show position isomers are those with a functional group or substituent that can be attached at different positions along the carbon chain, like alcohols (e.g., propan-1-ol and propan-2-ol), alkenes (e.g., 1-butene and 2-butene), and alkyl halides (e.g., 1-chloropropane and 2-chloropropane), where the position of the functional group changes while maintaining the same carbon skeleton.

Compounds that exhibit functional group isomerism include alcohols and ethers, aldehydes and ketones, and carboxylic acids and esters; essentially, any pair of compounds with different functional groups but the same molecular formula can be considered functional group isomers.

Compounds that show metamerism are typically ethers, secondary amines, and ketones; essentially, any compound with a functional group where different alkyl groups can be attached on either side, resulting in isomers with the same molecular formula but different structures around the functional group.

Compounds that exhibit cis-trans isomerism are typically alkenes (molecules with a carbon-carbon double bond), where the carbon atoms in the double bond each have two different groups attached to them, allowing for different spatial arrangements of those groups on either side of the double bond; a common example is "but-2-ene" which can exist as both cis and trans isomers depending on the position of the methyl groups relative to each other.

questions.explanation: Metamerism is a type of isomerism in which molecules have the same molecular formula but different alkyl groups around the functional group.For example,CH3-CH2-O-CH2-CH3 and CH3-O-CH2-CH2-CH3 are two metamers of each other.

questions.text: Which of the following pair groups belong to meta directing Groups?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: -NO2 and -SO3H are meta directing groups. The benzene ring has ortho positions(II and VI), para positions(IV), and meta positions(III and V). Those groups that are electrophile, also called meta-directing/deactivating groups, take electrons from the benzene ring towards themselves making the benzene to be deactivated. The benzene ring when deactivated directs the incoming groups to get attached to 3 and 5 positions which are meta positions.

questions.text: The rate of E1 reaction depends upon:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The rate of an E1 reaction depends solely on the concentration of the substrate because the rate-determining step involves the formation of a carbocation intermediate from the substrate.
The concentration of the nucleophile does not affect the rate of an E1 reaction, as the nucleophile is not involved in the rate-determining step. This is characteristic of SN1 and E1 reactions.

options.text: The concentration of both the nucleophile and the substrate
options.explanation: This scenario describes a bimolecular reaction, such as SN2, where both the substrate and nucleophile concentrations affect the rate. However, E1 reactions are unimolecular.

options.text: The amount of the solvent used
options.explanation: While the solvent can influence the reaction environment, it does not directly affect the rate of the E1 reaction, which is determined by the concentration of the substrate.

questions.explanation: The E1 reaction, or unimolecular elimination reaction, involves two key steps: the formation of a carbocation intermediate from the substrate and the subsequent elimination of a leaving group. The rate-determining step is the formation of the carbocation, which depends only on the concentration of the substrate. Therefore, the rate of the E1 reaction is independent of the nucleophile concentration, distinguishing it from bimolecular reactions like SN2, which depend on both substrate and nucleophile concentrations. While solvents can influence the reaction conditions and stability of intermediates, they do not directly affect the rate of an E1 reaction.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct IUPAC name?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: False. The substituents should be listed in alphabetical order, and this name incorrectly numbers the main chain.
False. The substituents are not listed in alphabetical order, and the numbering does not give the lowest numbers to the substituents.
False. The numbering does not provide the lowest possible numbers to the substituents.
True. This name correctly identifies the longest carbon chain (heptane) and numbers the substituents (methyl groups) at the lowest possible positions, 3 and 4.

questions.explanation: While naming a hydrocarbon, we take the longest carbon chain (heptane), then consider the additional groups (methyl), and finally number them from the one closest to the end of the main chain (3,4). Therefore, the IUPAC name is 3,4 - dimethylheptane.

questions.text: In SN1 reaction “1” stands for?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Polar solvents can stabilize the carbocation intermediate in SN1 reactions, but the '1' in SN1 does not refer to the solvent type. It refers to the molecularity of the rate-determining step.
The '1' in SN1 indicates that the rate-determining step involves only one molecule, making it unimolecular. This is the correct interpretation of the '1' in the mechanism.
In an SN2 reaction, the rate-determining step is bimolecular, involving two reacting species. However, this does not apply to SN1 reactions.
While the 'SN' in SN1 stands for 'nucleophilic substitution', the '1' refers to the unimolecular nature of the rate-determining step, not the nucleophile itself.

questions.explanation: The SN1 reaction is a substitution reaction in organic chemistry, the name of which refers to the Hughes-Ingold symbol of the mechanism. "SN" stands for "nucleophilic substitution", and the "1" says that the rate-determining step is unimolecular.

questions.text: CH3CH2CH2COCH3 is the functional isomer of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This compound is an aldehyde, which has the same molecular formula as the given ketone but a different functional group. Therefore, they are functional isomers.
This compound is an alcohol and does not have the same molecular formula as the given compound, hence they cannot be isomers.
This compound is a ketone like the given compound. Although they are positional isomers (same functional group, different position), they are not functional isomers.
This compound also has a ketone functional group and is not a functional isomer, as it shares the same functional group with the given compound.

questions.explanation: CH3CH2CH2COCH3 and CH3CH2CH2CH2CHO have the same number of atoms for the constituent elements, Carbon, Oxygen, and Hydrogen. However, the second requirement of a 'Functional Isomer' is that the functional groups, of the two isomers, must be different and since, in this case, we have a ketone functional group and an aldehyde functional, both compounds can be positively termed as 'Functional Isomers' of each other.Thus, option A is correct.

questions.text: IUPAC name of this is (CH3)2 - CH - CH (C2H5) - C(CH3)3 :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is the correct IUPAC name as it correctly identifies the longest carbon chain and the positions of the substituents.
This name incorrectly places the ethyl group at position 4 instead of 3, which does not follow the IUPAC naming rule of assigning the lowest locant numbers to substituents.
This option incorrectly places the ethyl group at position 5, which is not possible as it is not the smallest locant for the ethyl group.
This name incorrectly places the ethyl group at position 2, which does not reflect the actual structure of the compound.
This option incorrectly places the ethyl group at position 1, which would not be correct as it does not match the structure of the compound.

questions.explanation: The IUPAC name for the given compound (CH3)2-CH-CH(C2H5)-C(CH3)3 is "3-ethyl-2,2,4-trimethylpentane." Here's how the name is derived:1. Identify the longest continuous carbon chain, which in this case is a five-carbon chain.2. Number the carbon chain to give the substituents the lowest possible locants. In this case, we start numbering from the end nearest to the ethyl group, so the ethyl group is located on carbon 3.3. Assign substituent names and locants. The methyl groups attached to carbon 2 and carbon 4 are called "2,2-dimethyl" and "2,4-dimethyl," respectively. The ethyl group attached to carbon 3 is named "ethyl."4. Combine all the parts to form the complete IUPAC name: "3-ethyl-2,2,4-trimethylpentane."Therefore, the compound (CH3)2-CH-CH(C2H5)-C(CH3)3 is named 3-ethyl-2,2,4-trimethylpentane.

questions.text: (HCHO)nH20 is the chemical formula for?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Formic acid has the chemical formula CH₂O₂, which does not match the given formula. It is a simple carboxylic acid, not a polymer.
Paraformaldehyde is the polymerized form of formaldehyde and is represented by the formula (HCHO)nH₂O. It is a polymer created from the condensation of formaldehyde.
α-methyl propionaldehyde has the chemical formula C₄H₈O. It is an aldehyde, not a polymer of formaldehyde.
Propionic acid has the chemical formula C₃H₆O₂, which is a carboxylic acid and does not match the given formula.

questions.explanation: The compound (HCHO)nH₂O is known as paraformaldehyde. It is a polymer of formaldehyde, formed when multiple formaldehyde molecules undergo polymerization. This results in a series of repeating units, represented by (HCHO)n, with water (H₂O) being part of its stabilization in the polymer form. Options A, C, and D are incorrect because their chemical formulas do not match the structure of paraformaldehyde. Formic acid (Option A) is a simple carboxylic acid, α-methyl propionaldehyde (Option C) is an aldehyde with a different structure, and propionic acid (Option D) is another carboxylic acid.

questions.text: C6H5C≡N and C6H5N≡C exhibits which type of isomerism:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Functional group isomerism occurs when compounds with the same molecular formula have different functional groups. Here, the compounds differ in the arrangement of the carbon-nitrogen triple bond, classifying them as functional group isomers.

Optical isomerism involves isomers that differ in the way they rotate plane-polarized light due to the presence of chiral centers. This type of isomerism is not applicable here as there are no chiral centers in the given compounds.
Skeletal isomerism involves compounds with the same molecular formula but different arrangements of the carbon skeleton. The given compounds differ in functional groups, not skeletal structures.

Metamerism is a type of isomerism where compounds have the same molecular formula but differ in the alkyl chains on either side of a functional group. This is not applicable here as the difference lies in the functional group itself, not the side chains.

questions.explanation: Functional group isomerism is demonstrated by C6H5C≡N (benzonitrile) and C6H5N≡C (phenyl isocyanide) because they have the same molecular formula but different functional groups due to the distinct arrangement of the carbon-nitrogen triple bond. This arrangement leads to different chemical properties and reactivity.Other types of isomerism such as optical, skeletal, and metamerism are not applicable here because they involve different structural or stereochemical differences, or different arrangements of alkyl groups, which are not present in this case.

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Isomers are compounds with different physical and chemical properties but the same molecular formula. So options A and D are eliminated here.
Stereoisomers have identical molecular formulas and arrangements of atoms but differ in the orientation of these atoms in space. Only option B is correct.
Structural isomers have the same molecular formula but a different bonding arrangement among the atoms, and therefore a different structural formula, so option C and E are eliminated.
Isomers are compounds with different physical and chemical properties but the same molecular formula. So options A and D are eliminated here.
Structural isomers have the same molecular formula but a different bonding arrangement among the atoms, and therefore a different structural formula, so option C and E are eliminated.

questions.explanation: Isomers have the same molecular formula but different structures.

options.explanation: Ethene has 5 sigma bonds and 1 pi bond.

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Nucleophiles contain an unshared pair of electrons, and they look for a positive ‘centre’ to bond with.
An electrophile is an atom or molecule that is electron-deficient and seeks to accept electrons. It does not necessarily have an unshared pair of electrons.
This term is not commonly used in chemistry. It is not a recognized term for any specific chemical behavior.
This option is not correct.

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Pentane has three structural isomers.
This option is incorrect. Pentane has three structural isomers.
This option is incorrect. Pentane has three structural isomers.
Pentane has three structural isomers that are n-pentane, Iso-pentane (methyl butane), and neopentane (dimethylpropane).

questions.explanation: Pentane (C5H12) is an organic compound with five carbon atoms. Pentane has three isomers that are n-pentane, Iso-pentane (methyl butane) and neopentane (dimethylpropane). Therefore three isomers can be drawn from pentane.

questions.text: Which of the following is not the major source of organic compound?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Most hydrocarbons are extracted from crude oil and natural gas. Natural gas is mainly methane.

Petroleum is a naturally occurring, flammable liquid found within rock formations in the earth, and is comprised of a mixture of hydrocarbons and other organic compounds.The common organic compounds include alkanes (paraffins), naphthenes, aromatics, and heterocompounds

Coal consists carbon-based substances can be used as a source of specialty aromatic chemicals and aliphatic chemicals. Four widespread processes allow for making chemicals from coals: gasification, liquefaction, direct conversion, and co-production of chemicals and fuels along with electricity.

Ammoniacal liquor is an organic compound. It is a concentrated solution of ammonia, ammonium compounds, and sulfur compounds, obtained as a by-product in the destructive distillation of bituminous coal

questions.explanation: Ammoniacal liquor is an organic compound. It is a concentrated solution of ammonia, ammonium compounds, and sulfur compounds, obtained as a by-product in the destructive distillation of bituminous coal.

Shifting of proton within molecule is termed as tautomerism. C4H11N doesn't show tautomerism.
This option is incorrect as option a is correct.

questions.explanation: It gives metamerism which is when compounds having the same molecular formula but different alkyl groups on either side of functional groups.

questions.text: The number of chiral centres in a molecule 5-bromo 3-chloro hexan-2-ol is/are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: If there is only one chiral center in the molecule, it means there is only one carbon atom with four different substituents attached to it. A chiral center is also known as a stereocenter, and its presence indicates that the molecule is chiral and can have enantiomers (mirror-image isomers).

A chiral centre is an atom especially in an organic molecule that has four unique atoms or groups attached to it. To 3 carbons three different functional groups are attached. Therefore, 3 chiral centres are present.Note. To other adjacent carbon don't confuse the fact that 2 H are attached irrespective of different functional groups so they can't be considered chiral.
If there are two chiral centers in the molecule, it means there are two carbon atoms, each with four different substituents attached.
If there are five chiral centers in the molecule, it means there are five carbon atoms, each with four different substituents attached.

questions.explanation: A chiral centre is an atom especially in an organic molecule that has four unique atoms or groups attached to it. To 3 carbons three different functional groups are attached. Therefore, 3 chiral centers are present.Note. To other adjacent carbon don't confuse the fact that 2 H are attached irrespective of different functional groups so they can’t be considered chiral.

questions.text: Which one is a strong nucleophile:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: C6H5O, or phenoxide ion, is a weak nucleophile because the negative charge on the oxygen is delocalized through resonance with the aromatic ring, making the oxygen less nucleophilic. Hence, it is an incorrect option.
H-O- is a strong nucleophile as it has a negative charge and a small size, which allows it to easily donate its lone pair of electrons to form a bond with a positive or electrophilic atom or group.

NH3 is a weak nucleophile because it has a lone pair of electrons on the nitrogen atom, but it is not as basic as hydroxide ion or alkoxide ion. Therefore, it is less reactive than hydroxide ion and alkoxide ion as a nucleophile. Hence, it is an incorrect option.
C2H5O- is a weaker nucleophile than H-O- because it is a larger and less electronegative atom than oxygen, so it is less able to donate its electrons to form a bond. Hence, it is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: Nucleophilicity increases as the density of negative charge increases. An anion is always a better nucleophile than a neutral molecule, so the conjugate base is always a better nucleophile. A highly electronegative atom is a poor nucleophile because it is unwilling to share its electrons. A has ring system so increased charge density thus stronger nucleophile. H-O- is a strong nucleophile as it has a negative charge and a small size, which allows it to easily donate its lone pair of electrons to form a bond with a positive or electrophilic atom or group.

questions.text: Which of the following has the lowest vapour pressure at 20 degrees Centigrade?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Diethyl ether has a lower boiling point and weaker intermolecular forces compared to both water and chloroform. It is a volatile compound that readily evaporates, resulting in a higher vapor pressure than water and chloroform at 20 degrees Celsius.

Chloroform has weaker intermolecular forces than water. It is a polar molecule with a partial negative charge on the chlorine atom and partial positive charges on the hydrogen atoms. These dipole-dipole interactions result in a higher vapor pressure compared to water but lower than diethyl ether and carbon tetrachloride.

Carbon tetrachloride is a nonpolar molecule and has only weak dispersion forces between its molecules. It has the weakest intermolecular forces among the given substances, which allows its molecules to escape more easily and form a vapor. Thus, carbon tetrachloride has the highest vapor pressure at 20 degrees Celsius compared to water, chloroform, and diethyl ether.Carbon tetrachloride is a nonpolar molecule and has only weak dispersion forces between its molecules. It has the weakest intermolecular forces among the given substances, which allows its molecules to escape more easily and form a vapor. Thus, carbon tetrachloride has the highest vapor pressure at 20 degrees Celsius compared to water, chloroform, and diethyl ether.

Water has hydrogen bonding between its molecules, which leads to strong intermolecular forces. These forces hold the water molecules together tightly, making it more difficult for them to escape and form a vapor. Therefore, water has a relatively low vapor pressure at 20 degrees Celsius compared to the other substances.

questions.explanation: The boiling point of a substance is a measure of the strength of the intermolecular forces between the molecules of the substance. The stronger the intermolecular forces, the higher the boiling point. Water has strong hydrogen bonding interactions between the molecules, which is why it has the highest boiling point.The vapor pressure of a substance is the pressure exerted by the vapor of the substance above the liquid. The lower the vapor pressure, the less likely the molecules of the substance are to evaporate. Water has a low vapor pressure at 20 degrees Centigrade because the strong intermolecular forces make it difficult for the molecules to escape from the liquid.The other three substances, diethyl ether, chloroform, and carbon tetrachloride, have weaker intermolecular forces than water. This is why they have lower boiling points and higher vapor pressures at 20 degrees Centigrade.Therefore, water has the lowest vapor pressure at 20 degrees Celsius among the substances provided.

questions.text: Heavy water contains isotope of hydrogen:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Heavy water, also known as D2O, is a form of water in which the hydrogen atoms are replaced with deuterium, an isotope of hydrogen. Deuterium has one proton and one neutron in its nucleus, whereas the most common isotope of hydrogen, protium (option A), has only one proton.

Heavy water, also known as D2O, is a form of water in which the hydrogen atoms are replaced with deuterium, an isotope of hydrogen. Deuterium has one proton and one neutron in its nucleus, whereas the most common isotope of hydrogen, protium (option A), has only one proton.

Heavy water, also known as D2O, is a form of water in which the hydrogen atoms are replaced with deuterium, an isotope of hydrogen. Deuterium has one proton and one neutron in its nucleus, whereas the most common isotope of hydrogen, protium (option A), has only one proton.

Heavy water, also known as D2O, is a form of water in which the hydrogen atoms are replaced with deuterium, an isotope of hydrogen. Deuterium has one proton and one neutron in its nucleus, whereas the most common isotope of hydrogen, protium (option A), has only one proton.

questions.text: Identify the common name of the following compound:CH₃CH₂COCH₂OH
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: False. the common naming of a ketone, the two alkyl groups on both the sides of the carbonyl group are first identified, and then Greek letters are used for specifying the position of the carbon atoms near the carbonyl group.Let us now understand the concept of α, β, and γ carbons.α carbon= the one attached to carbonyl groupβ carbon= the one next to α carbonγ carbon= the one next to β carbonFor the compound in question, the hydroxy group is attached to the α carbon and the alkyl groups are ethyl (2 carbons on the left side) and methyl (1 carbon on the right side). Additionally, the alphabetical order is followed while writing alkyl groups. Hence, the common name of the compound becomes α-hydroxy-ethyl methyl ketone.
